---
author: ""
header-includes:
   - \usepackage{rotating}
   - \usepackage{float}
   - \usepackage{natbib}
   - \usepackage{booktabs}
   - \usepackage{multirow}
geometry: "left=1cm,right=1cm,top=0.9cm,bottom=1.2cm"
output: 
  pdf_document:
    citation_package: natbib
    keep_tex: true
    number_sections: true
#bibliography: ref.bib
link-citations: yes
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---


```{r, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
rm(list = ls())
options(xtable.comment = FALSE)
library(reshape)
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(naniar)
library(scales)
library(tidyr)
library(xtable)
library(pracma)
library(this.path)
setwd(this.path::this.dir())

# just choose combined for now
data <- readRDS("../../tmp/portfolio_results/by_liquidity_3x3/by_minus_spread_demeaned_ret1/1m_ret_by_3x3_scaled_summarized.RDS") %>%
  filter(var == "combined") %>%
  filter(var_type != "total") %>%
  select(-var) %>%
  mutate(coef = 100 * mean_ret1, se = 100 * se_ret1) %>%
  select(-mean_ret1, -se_ret1, -n) %>%
  setDT()

# order rows and columns
data <- data %>%
  mutate(row_idx = tidyr::replace_na(as.integer(target_liq_bin), 4)) %>%
  mutate(col_idx = tidyr::replace_na(as.integer(source_liq_bin), 4)) %>%
  mutate(col_idx = ifelse(var_type == "sym", col_idx, col_idx + 4)) %>%
  setDT()

# show sym | asy


# add stars
cuts <- abs(qnorm(c(.01, .05, .1) / 2))
data[, abs_tstat := abs(coef / se)]
data[, ss := ifelse(abs_tstat > cuts[1], 3, ifelse(abs_tstat > cuts[2], 2, ifelse(abs_tstat > cuts[3], 1, 0)))]
data[, coef_lab := paste0("$", sprintf("%.2f", coef))]
data[, ss_lab := sapply(data[, ss], function(x) {
  ifelse(x > 0,
    paste0("^{", strcat(rep("*", x)), "}$"),
    "$"
  )
})]
data[, coef_lab := paste0(coef_lab, ss_lab)]
data[, ss_lab := NULL]

est <- data %>%
  dcast(row_idx ~ col_idx, value.var = "coef_lab") %>%
  mutate(group_idx = 1, type = "coef")
est[is.na(est)] <- ""

se <- data %>%
  dcast(row_idx ~ col_idx, value.var = "se") %>%
  dplyr::mutate_at(vars(2:9), ~ paste0("$(", sprintf("%.2f", round(., 2)), ")$")) %>%
  mutate(group_idx = 2, type = "se")
se[se == "$(NA)$"] <- ""

tbl <- rbind(est, se) %>%
  arrange(row_idx, group_idx) %>%
  select(-group_idx, -type)
rm(est, se)

# add col columns
col_names <- data.table(t(as.data.frame(c("", paste0("(", 1:8, ")")))))
names(col_names) <- names(tbl)
tbl <- rbind(col_names, tbl)
rm(col_names)

# get labels right
tbl[, row_idx := c("", "Low", "", "Med", "", "High", "", "High - Low", "")]

# add another one
tt <- as.data.table(t(data.table(c("", rep(c("Low", "Med", "High", "High - Low"), 2)))))
names(tt) <- names(tbl)
tbl <- rbind(tt, tbl)
rm(tt)

align_vector <- paste0("ll", paste0(rep("c", times = ncol(tbl) - 1), collapse = ""))
x.side <- xtable(tbl, align = align_vector)

this_add_to_row <- list(
  pos = list(0, 1, nrow(tbl)-3, nrow(tbl)),
  command = c(
    "\\toprule \\ Lag firms & \\multicolumn{8}{c}{Lead firms} \\\\\n \\cmidrule(l){2-9} & \\multicolumn{4}{c}{Symmetric CSM} & \\multicolumn{4}{c}{Asymmetric CSM} \\\\\n \\cmidrule(l){2-5} \\cmidrule(l){2-9} ",
    "\\cmidrule(l){2-4} \\cmidrule(l){5-5} \\cmidrule(l){6-8} \\cmidrule(l){9-9} \\vspace{5pt}",
    "\\vspace{10pt}",
    "\\bottomrule \n"
  )
)

print(x.side,
  type = "latex",
  table.placement = "!ht",
  include.rownames = FALSE,
  include.colnames = FALSE,
  tabular.environment = "tabular",
  caption.placement = NULL, # "top", NULL
  hline.after = NULL, # We don't need hline; we use booktabs
  floating = FALSE, # whether \begin{Table} should be created (TRUE) or not (FALSE)
  sanitize.text.function = force, # Important to treat content of first column as latex function
  add.to.row = this_add_to_row
)

print(x.side,
  type = "latex",
  table.placement = "!ht",
  include.rownames = FALSE,
  include.colnames = FALSE,
  tabular.environment = "tabular",
  caption.placement = NULL, # "top", NULL
  hline.after = NULL, # We don't need hline; we use booktabs
  floating = FALSE, # whether \begin{Table} should be created (TRUE) or not (FALSE)
  sanitize.text.function = force, # Important to treat content of first column as latex function
  add.to.row = this_add_to_row,
  file = "liq_3x3_combined.tex"
)
```
